searchQuery: Multi-location franchised automotive dealership groups headquartered in the United States or Canada that operate service and fixed-operations departments across multiple rooftops. Their core business is selling and servicing new and used vehicles under franchised OEM brands; they consume inspection, documentation, and video-MPI software internally to run their service departments, they do not produce or sell automotive software. Strongest fit: regional groups with 5 to 50 rooftops with brand mix weighted toward Korean (Hyundai, Genesis) or luxury and German (Porsche, Audi, BMW, Mercedes, Land Rover, Volvo) franchises that carry high warranty and documentation burden, similar to LA Car Guy, McGovern Automotive Group, Earnhardt Auto Centers, and Koons Automotive. Geographic scope: headquartered in the United States or Canada. EXCLUDE automotive software vendors, dealer management system providers, video-MPI and inspection-app companies, smart-glasses companies, and vehicle OEMs, including but not limited to: Plasma, TruVideo, XTime, Cox Automotive, DealerFX, AutoPoint, Solera, MyKaarma, CDK, Reynolds and Reynolds, Tekion, Dealertrack. Also exclude single-rooftop independent dealers and used-car-only lots.

criteria.description: Operates as a multi-location automotive dealership group with two or more franchised new-vehicle rooftops
Operates service and fixed-operations (repair / multi-point inspection) departments at its dealerships
Headquartered in the United States or Canada
Carries franchised new-vehicle OEM brands (not a used-car-only or independent operator)
Brand mix includes Korean (Hyundai/Genesis) or luxury/German franchises, OR documented high warranty/service-documentation activity

enrichments.title: Rooftop count
enrichments.instructions: Number of dealership locations (rooftops) the group operates.

enrichments.title: Franchised OEM brands
enrichments.instructions: List the franchised new-vehicle OEM brands the group sells/services (e.g., Toyota, Hyundai, Genesis, BMW, Porsche).

enrichments.title: Headquarters
enrichments.instructions: City and state/province of group headquarters.

enrichments.title: Employee / technician count
enrichments.instructions: Approximate total employees and/or number of service technicians if available.

enrichments.title: DMS provider
enrichments.instructions: Dealer management system in use if identifiable (CDK, Reynolds & Reynolds, Tekion, Dealertrack).

enrichments.title: MPI / video inspection tool
enrichments.instructions: Current multi-point-inspection / video tool if identifiable (XTime, AutoPoint, DealerFX, MyKaarma, TruVideo, CDK); note any publicly stated media-attach rate.

enrichments.title: AI / digital adoption posture
enrichments.instructions: Evidence of AI/automation adoption in service: voice-AI vendors (Mia, Pam), OEM AR-glasses programs, digital-service case studies, innovation press.

enrichments.title: Service / fixed-ops hiring
enrichments.instructions: Count and titles of open service technician, service advisor, or fixed-operations roles; cite the careers page or job board URL.

enrichments.title: Ownership / status
enrichments.instructions: Private, family-owned, PE-backed, or publicly traded; name parent or PE firm if applicable.

enrichments.title: Company LinkedIn URL
enrichments.instructions: The group's company LinkedIn profile URL including https:// prefix.

enrichments.title: Recent service signal
enrichments.instructions: Recent news on OEM video-MPI mandate exposure, warranty documentation, fixed-ops expansion, or group acquisition.

scoring_model.scale: 0-5 per axis; composite = weighted blend, rounded to one decimal; tier from composite (high >=3.8, med 2.6-3.7, low <2.6)

axes.name: Documentation & Warranty Burden
axes.primary_input: Franchised OEM brand mix + cited OEM documentation/video mandate

rubric.5: Majority (>50%) Korean (Hyundai/Genesis/Kia) or luxury-German (BMW/Mercedes/Porsche/Audi/Land Rover/Volvo) rooftops, OR a cited named OEM photo/video documentation mandate. REQUIRES cited evidence (WOW_EVIDENCE_SHAPE).
rubric.3: Domestic-heavy mix (Ford/Stellantis/GM) with documented warranty volume (these brands deny first-time claims on technicalities)
rubric.2: Mostly mainstream import (Toyota/Honda/Subaru/Nissan), limited high-burden exposure
rubric.0: Used-only / no franchised new-vehicle warranty work

axes.name: MPI Tooling Gap (Displacement)
axes.primary_input: Current MPI/video tool maturity + AI-adoption posture (attach rate only if publicly cited)

rubric.5: Legacy/limited or no MPI-video tooling AND a cited dissatisfaction or low-attach signal: wide-open displacement
rubric.4: Legacy MPI tool in place (XTime/AutoPoint/DealerFX/MyKaarma), no modern automation layer
rubric.3: Legacy tool + active AI adoption (voice AI / OEM AR pilots): receptive but partially served
rubric.2: Modern, well-tooled operation (e.g., Tekion + high attach) open to an integration play
rubric.1: Best-in-class, fully tooled; glasses purely incremental

axes.name: Hiring (Fixed-Ops Growth)
axes.primary_input: Count of open service/fixed-ops roles matched by HIRING_KEYWORD_REGEX (Phase 6j job data)

rubric.1: No current openings but recent fixed-ops expansion

scoring_model.WOW_EVIDENCE_SHAPE: Cited evidence that a target carries Korean or luxury/German franchises (implying 50%+ warranty mix), OR a named OEM photo/video documentation mandate, OR a publicly documented media-attach rate well under the 90% industry north-star.
